info.title: Fundraising
info.description: This API manages information contained within the fundraising hierarchy and related entities such as campaigns, funds, and appeals.

get.description: Returns information about the appeal with the specified ID.

parameters.name: appeal_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the appeal for which information is to be retrieved.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains the specified appeal entity.

400.description: Returned when the specified appeal ID in the request URL is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the appeal.

404.description: Returned when the specified appeal is not found.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of appeals.

The default sorting behavior is to list appeals in ascending order based on when they were created.

However, the last_modified parameter overrides the default sorting behavior to list appeals in ascending order based on when they were last modified. The last_modified parameter also adds the sort_token parameter to the next_link URL to ensure that appeals are stably sorted and that order persists when changes occur while working through a paginated list.

If the last_modified and date_added parameters are both specified, the sorting behavior is to list appeals based on when they were last modified.

parameters.name: date_added
parameters.description: Represents a filter for appeals created on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

parameters.name: last_modified
parameters.description: Represents a filter for appeals modified on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

parameters.name: sort_token
parameters.description: Represents a token filter to provide the next stable-sorted list of appeals. This will be provided on the next_link collection response property when last_modified or sort_token filters are specified on the request.

parameters.name: include_inactive
parameters.description: Set this parameter to "true" to include inactive appeals in the response.

parameters.name: appeal_id
parameters.description: Represents a list of appeal identifiers. Returns appeals if any of the specified appeal identifiers match any of their appeals.  For example, "appeal_id=280&appeal_id=1232" returns appeals with either "280" or "1232" appeal identifiers.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to return. The default is 500. The maximum is 5000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to skip. For use with pagination.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of appeal entities.

value.description: Annual Newsletter
Celebrity Auction
Year End Appeal

400.description: Returned when the specified request parameters are not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the appeals.

get.description: Returns a list of appeal categories.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of string.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the appeal categories.

get.description: Returns a list of custom fields for the appeal with the specified ID.

parameters.name: appeal_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the appeal for which the custom fields are to be retrieved.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of custom field entities.

400.description: Returned when the specified appeal ID in the request URL is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the custom fields.

404.description: Returned when the specified appeal is not found.

requestBody.description: An object representing the custom field to add.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds and a new custom field has been created. The response body contains the ID of the newly added custom field.

400.description: Returned when the request body is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to create the custom field.

404.description: Returned when the specified appeal is not found.

delete.description: Deletes the custom field with the specified ID.

parameters.name: custom_field_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the custom field to be deleted.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds and the custom field has been deleted.

400.description: Returned when the specified custom field ID in the request URL is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to delete the custom field.

parameters.name: custom_field_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the custom field to be edited.

requestBody.description: An object representing updated properties of the custom field to add.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds and the custom field has been edited.

400.description: Returned when the request body is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to edit the custom field.

404.description: Returned when the specified custom field is not found.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of custom field category entities.

get.description: Gets the list of custom field category values.

parameters.name: category_name
parameters.description: The name of the category for which the values should be returned.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of string custom field category values.

get.description: Returns information about the campaign with the specified ID.

parameters.name: campaign_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the campaign for which information is to be retrieved.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains the specified campaign entity.

400.description: Returned when the specified campaign ID in the request URL is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the campaign.

404.description: Returned when the specified campaign is not found.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of campaigns.

The default sorting behavior is to list campaigns in ascending order based on when they were created.

However, the last_modified parameter overrides the default sorting behavior to list campaigns in ascending order based on when they were last modified. The last_modified parameter also adds the sort_token parameter to the next_link URL to ensure that campaigns are stably sorted and that order persists when changes occur while working through a paginated list.

If the last_modified and date_added parameters are both specified, the sorting behavior is to list campaigns based on when they were last modified.

parameters.name: date_added
parameters.description: Represents a filter for campaigns created on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

parameters.name: last_modified
parameters.description: Represents a filter for campaigns modified on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

parameters.name: sort_token
parameters.description: Represents a token filter to provide the next stable-sorted list of campaigns. This will be provided on the next_link collection response property when last_modified or sort_token filters are specified on the request.

parameters.name: include_inactive
parameters.description: Set this parameter to "true" to include inactive campaigns in the response.

parameters.name: campaign_id
parameters.description: Represents a list of campaign identifiers. Returns campaigns if any of the specified campaign identifiers match any of their campaigns.  For example, "campaign_id=280&campaign_id=1232" returns campaigns with either "280" or "1232" campaign identifiers.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to return. The default is 500. The maximum is 5000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to skip. For use with pagination.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of campaign entities.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of funds.

The default sorting behavior is to list funds in ascending order based on when they were created.

However, the last_modified parameter overrides the default sorting behavior to list funds in ascending order based on when they were last modified. The last_modified parameter also adds the sort_token parameter to the next_link URL to ensure that funds are stably sorted and that order persists when changes occur while working through a paginated list.

If the last_modified and date_added parameters are both specified, the sorting behavior is to list funds based on when they were last modified.

parameters.name: date_added

get.description: Returns information about the package with the specified ID.

parameters.name: package_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the package for which information is to be retrieved.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains the specified package entity.

example.notes: Millennium Letter from the Director of Development.

400.description: Returned when the specified package ID in the request URL is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the package.

404.description: Returned when the specified package is not found.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of packages.

The default sorting behavior is to list packages in ascending order based on when they were created.

However, the last_modified parameter overrides the default sorting behavior to list packages in ascending order based on when they were last modified. The last_modified parameter also adds the sort_token parameter to the next_link URL to ensure that packages are stably sorted and that order persists when changes occur while working through a paginated list.

If the last_modified and date_added parameters are both specified, the sorting behavior is to list packages based on when they were last modified.

parameters.name: appeal_id
parameters.description: Represents a list of appeal identifiers. Returns packages if any of the specified appeal identifiers match any of the package's associated constituents. For example, "appeal_id=280&appeal_id=1232" returns packages with either "280" or "1232" appeal identifiers.

parameters.name: date_added
parameters.description: Represents a filter for packages created on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

parameters.name: include_inactive
parameters.description: Set this parameter to "true" to include inactive appeals in the response.

parameters.name: last_modified
parameters.description: Represents a filter for packages modified on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

parameters.name: sort_token
parameters.description: Represents a token filter to provide the next stable-sorted list of packages. This will be provided on the next_link collection response property when last_modified or sort_token filters are specified on the request.

parameters.name: package_id
parameters.description: Represents a list of package identifiers. Returns packages if any of the specified package identifiers match any of their packages.  For example, "package_id=280&package_id=1232" returns packages with either "280" or "1232" package identifiers.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to return. The default is 500. The maximum is 5000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to skip. For use with pagination.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of package entities.

value.description: Newsletter Envelope
value.notes: Include special membership forms in the newsletter mailing.  Responses will be marked with NWSLT.

value.description: Millennium Letter
value.notes: Millennium Letter from the Director of Development.

value.description: Electronic Invitation to the Open House

400.description: Returned when the specified request parameters are not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the packages.

id.description: The immutable system record ID of the appeal.

category.description: The category of the appeal. Available values are the entries in the Appeal Category table.

date_added.description: The date when the appeal was created. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

date_modified.description: The date when the appeal was last modified. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

description.description: The display name of the appeal.

end_date.description: The end date of the appeal. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-12-25T10:29:43.

inactive.description: This computed field indicates that the appeal is active if the current date is after any start_date and before any end_date.

lookup_id.description: The user-defined identifier for the appeal.

start_date.description: The start date of the appeal. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

AppealRead.description: Appeals are solicitations to generate gifts, such as direct mailings, online donation pages, phonathons, auctions, and events.

value.description: The monetary value.

Currency.description: For consistency, currency is configured at the organization level. This ensures that all monetary amounts are consistent, regardless of where they are entered or viewed.

count.description: The number of items available for retrieval into the collection after applying any request parameters. The limit and offset parameters do not affect the count, but to facilitate paging, they may affect the number of items in the value result set.

next_link.description: For paginated responses, the URI for the next page of results.

value.description: The set of items included in the response. This may be a subset of the items in the collection.

ApiCollection_AppealRead_.description: Fetching data all at once quickly becomes unmanageable. The collection entity helps by returning paginated chunks of large data sets. This entity includes helpful metadata and properties to paginate and iterate through the data.

next_link.description: For paginated responses, the URI for the next page of results.

value.description: The set of items included in the response. This may be a subset of the items in the collection.

ApiCollection_CustomFieldRead_.description: Fetching data all at once quickly becomes unmanageable. The collection entity helps by returning paginated chunks of large data sets. This entity includes helpful metadata and properties to paginate and iterate through the data.

id.description: The immutable system record ID of the custom field.

category.description: The custom field category. Available values are the entries in the Custom Field Categories table of the parent object.

comment.description: The comment on the custom field.

date.description: The date on the custom field. Uses ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43.

date_added.description: The date when the custom field was created. The date includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

date_modified.description: The date when the custom field was last modified. The date includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

type.description: The type of data that the custom field represents. Available values are listed below.

value.description: The value of the custom field. The type property determines the format.

CustomFieldRead.description: While records provide many fields to track information, organizations often require additional details. To track this specialized information, use custom fields.
CustomFieldRead.x-additional-info:
Available values for the type of a custom field:

Type Description
Boolean Defines the custom field as a boolean value.
CodeTableEntry Defines the custom field as a string value that represents a code table entry description.
ConstituentId Defines the custom field as a constituent identifier value.
Currency Defines the custom field as a decimal value.
Date Defines the custom field as a date value in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.
FuzzyDate Defines the custom field as a fuzzy date value.
Number Defines the custom field as an integer value.
Text Defines the custom field as a string value. Character limit: 255.

value.description: The value of the custom field. The type property determines the format. Character limit depends on data type. Number types only support values greater than 0.

ApiCollection_AttachmentRead_.description: Fetching data all at once quickly becomes unmanageable. The collection entity helps by returning paginated chunks of large data sets. This entity includes helpful metadata and properties to paginate and iterate through the data.

id.description: The immutable system record ID of the attachment.

content_type.description: The content type. For physical attachments only.

date.description: The date of the attachment. Uses ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

file_name.description: The file name. For physical attachments only.

file_size.description: The file size in bytes. For physical attachments only.

parent_id.description: The parent object's immutable system record ID.

tags.description: The tags associated with the attachment. Tags supplement the attachment’s name and description and identify it based on how an organization categorizes attachments. Available values are the entries in the Document Tags table.

thumbnail_id.description: The identifier of the thumbnail. For physical attachments that are images only.

thumbnail_url.description: The URL for a thumbnail. For physical attachments that are images only. Contains a time-bound signature that limits access to 30 minutes.

type.description: The attachment type. Available values are Link and Physical. Physical attachments are uploaded files such as images, PDFs, or Word documents that are saved locally or on the network. They are stored and managed in the system. Link attachments are links to files such as images, blog posts, or YouTube videos that are online or in a cloud storage account. They are stored and managed externally.

url.description: The URL for the attachment. The URL for a physical attachment contains a time-bound signature that limits access to 30 minutes.

AttachmentRead.description: Many cultivation activities collect related collateral, such as correspondence and prospect research. Attachments can save this data on the appropriate record to maintain a complete view of those activities.

date.description: The date of the attachment. Uses ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00. This property defaults to the current date and time if not supplied.

file_id.description: The identifier of the file. Character limit: 36. For physical attachments only.

file_name.description: The name of the file. Character limit: 255. For physical attachments only.

name.description: The name of the attachment. Character limit: 150.

parent_id.description: The parent object's immutable system record ID.

tags.description: The tags associated with the attachment. Tags supplement the attachment’s name and description and identify it based on how an organization categorizes attachments. Available values are the entries in the Document Tags table. Character limit: 100.

thumbnail_id.description: The identifier of the thumbnail. Character limit: 36. For physical attachments only.

type.description: The attachment type. Available values are Link and Physical. Physical attachments are uploaded files such as images, PDFs, or Word documents that are saved locally or on the network. They are stored and managed in the system. Link attachments are links to files such as images, blog posts, or YouTube videos that are online or in a cloud storage account. They are stored and managed externally.

url.description: The URL for the attachment. This is required for link attachments but does not apply to physical attachments.

AttachmentAdd.description: Many cultivation activities collect related collateral, such as correspondence and prospect research. Attachments can save this data on the appropriate record to maintain a complete view of those activities.

date.description: The date of the attachment. Uses ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00. This property cannot be set to null.

name.description: The name of the attachment. Character limit: 150.

tags.description: The tags associated with the attachment. Tags supplement the attachment’s name and description and identify it based on how an organization categorizes attachments. Available values are the entries in the Document Tags table. Character limit: 100.

url.description: The URL for the attachment. You can only edit the URL for link attachments. This property cannot be set to null.

AttachmentEdit.description: Many cultivation activities collect related collateral, such as correspondence and prospect research. Attachments can save this data on the appropriate record to maintain a complete view of those activities.

value.description: The set of items included in the response. This may be a subset of the items in the collection.

constituent_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the target constituent.

fundraiser_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the fundraiser assigned to the constituent.

start.description: The fundraiser start date. Uses ISO-8601 format: 2017-01-29T00:00:00.

type.description: The type of fundraiser. Available values are the entries in the Solicitor Type table.

FundraiserAssignmentRead.description: Fundraiser constituents interact with other constituents on behalf of your organization to cultivate relationships and request donations. Fundraiser assignments allow you to assign specific constituents to fundraisers for solicitation purposes.

date_added.description: The date when the campaign was created. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

date_modified.description: The date when the campaign was last modified. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

end_date.description: The end date of the campaign. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-12-25T10:29:43.

lookup_id.description: The user-defined identifier for the campaign.

start_date.description: The start date of the campaign. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

CampaignRead.description: Campaigns are your organization’s overall fundraising efforts or initiatives, such as operating expenses, new buildings, and endowments.

NewDocumentInfo.description: Cultivation activities often result in physical collateral such as images, PDFs, or Word files. The New Document entity allows you to upload these files to maintain a holistic view of target constituents.

FileDefinition.description: Contains metadata for uploading a document and unique document identifier for physical attachments.
